prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿跳" (single-leg hop) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person mid-flight during a single-leg horizontal hop: right leg fully extended just having pushed off the ground; left leg held off the ground throughout (single-leg drill); body traveling forward with slight forward lean; arms in opposition for momentum; torso upright; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles and quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the hopping leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group and core muscles for stabilization. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "小腿 / 股四头肌" pointing to the calf and quadriceps of the hopping leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "腘绳肌 / 核心" pointing to the hamstring and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
